word: G4014 - περιαιρέω - periairéō - per-ee-ahee-reh'-o - Take away

sections.paragraph:
- Original: περιαιρέω

- Transliteration: Periaireo

- Phonetic: per-ee-ahee-reh'-o

- Definition:

1. to take away that which surrounds or envelopes a thing

2. metaph. to take away altogether or entirely

a. the guilt of sin, to expiate perfectly

- Origin: from G4012 and G138 (including its alternate)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4012 and G138 (including its alternate); to remove all around that is unveil cast off (anchor); figuratively to expiate: - take away (up).
